[2018-05-09 05:24] amalloy: is the "claim draw" button always present for both players, or can it appear for just one? i was surprised to see it appear for me when i had a forced win, but i was against a casual-match bot so i can't find out if a real opponent could have claimed a draw
[2018-05-09 05:26] apooche: It doesn’t appear for both at the same time
[2018-05-09 05:26] apooche: It appears for you if your opponent isn’t making progress
[2018-05-09 05:26] amalloy: what counts as progress?
[2018-05-09 05:26] mrguy888: it can appear for both at the same time
[2018-05-09 05:27] mrguy888: if both players space space from the start they both get a button
[2018-05-09 05:27] apooche: Don’t think that is true mrguy, doesn’t it always appear at the start of your turn?
[2018-05-09 05:27] mrguy888: well you can choose not to press it
[2018-05-09 05:28] apooche: But it will always appear for one player before the other
[2018-05-09 05:28] mrguy888: oh i see what you mean
[2018-05-09 05:28] apooche: I guess the definition of ‘appear’
[2018-05-09 05:28] liadahlia (Lia): What is the condition for the draw button to pop up anyways?
[2018-05-09 05:29] apooche: Opponent not making progress
[2018-05-09 05:29] apooche: It isn’t a simple condition
[2018-05-09 05:29] mrguy888: No false positives and pretty quick to appear.
[2018-05-09 05:30] liadahlia (Lia): So the game draws immediately as soon as one player press it?
[2018-05-09 05:30] liadahlia (Lia): How does it guard against false positives?
[2018-05-09 05:31] liadahlia (Lia): I don't really know if there is one
[2018-05-09 05:31] apooche: I mean I don’t have the draw detect code
[2018-05-09 05:32] apooche: The only “false positives” you will see are positions where you have the win (and the button only appears for you), or positions where opponent is griefing
[2018-05-09 05:34] apooche: Will wrote the draw detect algorithm a long time ago though and nobody has observed any problems with it in all this time.
[2018-05-09 05:34] amalloy: well, there's certainly at least one exception to that, as noted at  https://www.reddit.com/r/Prismata/comments/2pzhz8/i_won_a_game_but_for_some_reason_my_opponent_was/cn220on/
i won a game but for some reason my opponent was able to claim a d...
Hey guys, I investigated this and in the game originally posted here, "Claim Draw" was allowed due to a bug in my code. I did not intend for a...
[2018-05-09 05:35] apooche: That thread is 3 years old
[2018-05-09 05:36] amalloy: i'm not saying the bug is still present, i'm disagreeing with "no problems observed with it ever"
[2018-05-09 05:37] amalloy: anyway, it sounds like we don't have an explicit definition for "not making progress" but we think the engine is always right about it. good to know
[2018-05-09 05:38] apooche: I mean really I’m just too lazy to type up my understanding of it on my phone right now
[2018-05-09 05:38] liadahlia (Lia): I think the potential for false positive makes it scary
[2018-05-09 05:39] apooche: And I guess 3 years ago is when Will explained how it works, so that was what I was thinking of when I said he wrote it a long time ago
[2018-05-09 05:39] liadahlia (Lia): In the future, a new unit cause a new type of draw condition to occur, and with it new false positives
[2018-05-09 05:39] apooche: But I could have worded it clearer
[2018-05-09 05:39] liadahlia (Lia): There's a potential for that
[2018-05-09 05:40] amalloy: i also remember reading that thread three years ago and not loving the response "the game only offers a draw after TEN turns if you are storing up Green or Gold" because it seems like there must be positions where you need to save up 40 green for a massive cluster volley to win
[2018-05-09 05:40] amalloy: like, i have 1 conduit and my opponent has...i dunno, 25 husks?
[2018-05-09 05:41] liadahlia (Lia): Yeah a new unit like cluster might add new false positives
[2018-05-09 05:41] apooche: Yeah the classic puzzle is to demonstrate that there are positions where you need to store 40 green to win
[2018-05-09 05:41] liadahlia (Lia): Is it possible to draw by elimination?
[2018-05-09 05:42] amalloy: well. i'm not sure actually
[2018-05-09 05:42] miuna6933 (Aka): if you have only gauss charges, and you kill your opponent's last thing
[2018-05-09 05:42] amalloy: i think if you end your turn with no units on your side you lose
[2018-05-09 05:42] miuna6933 (Aka): you both have nothing left
[2018-05-09 05:42] liadahlia (Lia): You have 1 pixie left and opponent has an engi
[2018-05-09 05:43] liadahlia (Lia): @Apooche you have any ideas on that one?
[2018-05-09 05:44] apooche: Is that what you are asking?
[2018-05-09 05:44] apooche: The game warns you that it will happen
[2018-05-09 05:44] liadahlia (Lia): You have 1 pixie opp have 1 engi
[2018-05-09 05:45] liadahlia (Lia): Game warns you if you click last pixie?
[2018-05-09 05:45] apooche: Yeah that is a draw. When you go to confirm phase it warns, like a breach warning
[2018-05-09 05:45] apooche: If they had 2 engis, it warns you that you’ll lose
[2018-05-09 05:47] miuna6933 (Aka): oh yeah i got the "loss imminent" once, in a puzzle i think

[2018-05-09 09:41] awaclus: if it absorbs even once, it's good
[2018-05-09 09:42] liadahlia (Lia): well, it depends on how much you absorb
[2018-05-09 09:43] liadahlia (Lia): but yeah, par is thunderhead dying on lifespan 2
[2018-05-09 09:43] Deleted User (Deleted User): I'm stuck on the Unfair Challenge
[2018-05-09 09:43] awaclus: but like if it absorbs once and then soaks the next turn, it probably absorbs enough
[2018-05-09 09:43] liadahlia (Lia): that's what your opponent usually hopes for
[2018-05-09 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): thunderhead is nasty because it forces your opponent to go out of his way to race for 11 attack
[2018-05-09 09:44] liadahlia (Lia): even if it's not optimal
[2018-05-09 09:44] awaclus: even if it just ends up being soak, it's not terrible if it fits your build
[2018-05-09 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): thunderhead is pretty bad against units that lets you cheat out attacks though
[2018-05-09 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): like thermite or cluster
[2018-05-09 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): bloodrager is good yeah, but more because it is absorb denial against thunderhead
[2018-05-09 09:45] liadahlia (Lia): but yeah thead also suffers from absorb denial
[2018-05-09 09:46] liadahlia (Lia): i *think* if thead dies without absorbing at all it's pretty bad
[2018-05-09 09:46] p0lari (Polari): if you manage to buy a thunderhead late so that it's 4 real damage and 11 soak without getting exploited it's good
[2018-05-09 09:46] liadahlia (Lia): if you can manage that somehow
[2018-05-09 09:46] p0lari (Polari): the bad thunderheads are the early ones where their attack gets absorbed and the opponent can just keep building econ
[2018-05-09 09:46] liadahlia (Lia): you're in a pretty commanding lead if you're able to do that already, so kinda win-more
[2018-05-09 09:47] p0lari (Polari): or worse, sell off their attack as mentioned
[2018-05-09 09:47] darkeisbein: well depends if your opponent just econs up to avoid thead absorb, far more then is reasonable thead might still be good
[2018-05-09 09:47] p0lari (Polari): ones that immediately get hit for precisely 11 aren't too hot either
[2018-05-09 09:47] liadahlia (Lia): if a unit forces opponent to go for an inoptimal build, then it's probably pretty good
[2018-05-09 09:48] liadahlia (Lia): but yeah you never buy thead if opponent can setup exactly 11 when he's built
